hypothesis: Incorporating explicit solvent molecule interactions through a hybrid implicit-explicit solvation encoding in the graph augmentation scheme—representing first-shell water or co-solvent molecules as auxiliary nodes with learned interaction edges—will improve LoQI's conformer accuracy for polar, hydrogen-bond-rich drug-like molecules by better capturing solvent-induced conformational preferences that the current CPCM-only implicit solvent model systematically underestimates, as evidenced by a measurable reduction in RMSD to crystal structures for molecules with high polar surface area compared to the implicit-solvent-only baseline.
